description: Use this skill whenever a user asks how AI changes a workflow, role, journey, RACI, service blueprint, operating model, or strategy — including any version of "what should AI do vs humans?", "what should we automate?", "who's accountable when the AI is wrong?", or "what evals/telemetry/release gates do we need before launch?". Trigger on prompts about: redesigning incident response, customer support, legal review, security/SOC triage, sales, or operations workflows for AI; designing AI-assisted/agentic features and the human oversight around them; writing strategy memos or operating-model proposals about AI changing a team's work; building RACI-like ownership matrices, service blueprints, or journey maps that involve AI somewhere in the flow; defining AI-era personas (role-responsibility views, not marketing personas); translating trust requirements into evals, telemetry, and release gates. Use it even when the user does not say "human responsibility mapping" — they almost never will. The cue is any question about the boundary between human and AI responsibility, accountability, or work redesign.

results.query: I'm doing roadmap planning for Q3 and need to write a strategy memo about how AI changes our customer-success org. Specifically: which CSM activities get automated, which stay human, and what the new operating model looks like. about 2 pages.

ok we're rolling out an AI summarizer for our incident response slack channel. PagerDuty fires, we want claude to summarize the thread, suggest next steps, eventually maybe draft the postmortem. how should we think about what stays human vs what AI does

we're a legal team adding an AI contract reviewer (Harvey-like). Need to figure out RACI for the new workflow. Right now it's: associate reviews → senior reviews → partner approves. With the AI in the loop, who's accountable when it misses a clause?

Our radiologists are piloting an AI triage tool for chest x-rays. PM here. I need to figure out what the AI can do safely vs what stays with the radiologist, and what evals/telemetry we need before broader rollout. Regulated, FDA cleared device.

I'm building a service blueprint for our new agent-assisted onboarding flow. Need to figure out what changes vs the current human-only flow — handoffs, reviews, when humans step in. Help me think through it.

what should we automate in our SOC alert triage and what should the analysts keep doing themselves? we have 200+ alerts/day, half are false positives. trying to figure out where to put AI without burning out the team or missing real attacks
write me a marketing persona for our new dev-tools SaaS product. Target is mid-market backend engineers, pain points around CI/CD, JTBD around faster deploys.
I have a CSV with 50k customer records and I want to segment them into clusters for an email campaign. Can you do k-means in pandas and give me the segment profiles?
Our React app's user-profile page is slow to load on mobile. Can you help me profile it and figure out where the bottleneck is? probably hydration related.
I want to implement a basic RACI matrix for my product team — PM, EM, designer, engineers. Just normal team responsibilities. Can you help me lay it out?
Help me design a journey map for the new self-serve signup flow. Acquisition → activation → first value moment → retention. No AI involved, just trying to map user emotion at each step.
we're rebuilding our onboarding wizard and want to A/B test two flows: short (3 steps) vs guided (7 steps). Help me set up the experiment and define success metrics.
